title: crepis sonchifolia
intro: Crepis sonchifolia, also known as Babchi, is a plant species in the dandelion family that is native to Asia and is used in traditional medicine. It has been found to have medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits including wound healing, skin protection, and the treatment of various skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant's roots and leaves can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, and its seeds contain a pigment called psoralen, which is used in traditional Indian medicine to treat skin conditions. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is valued for its ability to promote skin health and is often used to prepare topical preparations and internal remedies for skin conditions and other health issues.
